items.title: LIVE: French President Emmanuel Macron visits cyclone-devastated Mayotte
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Macron arrived in the Indian Ocean archipelago of Mayotte to survey the devastation wreaked by Cyclone Chido. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4gjfn1F

#mayotte #macron #live

items.title: Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen’s Houthi-held capital and port city
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A series of intense Israeli airstrikes shook Yemen’s rebel-held capital Sanaa and a port city early Thursday and killed at least nine people, officials said, shortly after a Houthi missile targeted central Israel. Read more here: https://bit.ly/49M0TVt

items.title: LIVE: French court declares ex-husband of Gisèle Pelicot guilty of rapes and all other charges
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A French court on Thursday declared the ex-husband of Gisèle Pelicot guilty of aggravated rapes and all other charges against him. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3DoWqfc

#france #trial #live

items.title: LIVE: Russian cosmonauts conduct spacewalk outside the International Space Station
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Watch live as Roscosmos cosmonauts Alexey Ovchinin and Ivan Vagner venture outside the International Space Station to conduct a spacewalk.
Credit: NASA

#spacewalk #space #roscosmos #internationalspacestation #news

items.title: LIVE: Outside court as Luigi Mangione accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing appears at hearing
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
AP is live outside a court in Hollidaysburg as Luigi Mangione, the suspect charged with the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO, is being taken to hearings on related Pennsylvania criminal charges and efforts to extradite him to New York.
Mangione faces a preliminary hearing on forgery and firearms charges, as well as consideration of a fugitive from justice complaint. Read more: https://bit.ly/4iFhbDv 

#luigimangione #brianthompson #unitedhealthcare #pennsylvania #usa #news #live

items.title: Separated for decades, Assad's fall spurs hope for families split by Golan Heights buffer zone
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
When Israel seized the strategic Golan Heights from Syria in 1967, many families were separated by a buffer zone created between the Israeli-controlled region and the rest of Syria. Some families in Majdal Shams, a mountain town populated by members of the Druze religious community, haven't seen their loved ones in decades. But the fall of Syrian President Bashar Assad has spurred hope that family meetings might be allowed. Read more: https://bit.ly/4gmCXKC

#assad #syria #golanheights #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Survivors of a cyclone that tore through the French island territory of Mayotte said Thursday that they expected the storm's death toll to rise.  (AP Video by Sam Mednick)

items.title: Germany hoping the thousands of Syrian doctors working in the country stay after Assad's fall
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Thousands of Syrian doctors work in Germany and the fall of Bashar Assad is raising concern over the potential consequences for the health sector if many of them were to return home.

#assad #syria #germany #news

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Dogs dress up for canine carnival in Lagos
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A canine carnival in Lagos, Nigeria is giving dog lovers in the country a chance to celebrate their pets. The dogs, some of which are in fancy dress, are blessed by a priest and the organisers hope to show the world that Nigeria is a nation that cherishes canines.

items.title: AP reporter at the scene of massive destruction from Cyclone Chido
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
French authorities said at least 31 people have died and more than 1,500 people were injured, more than 200 critically after Cyclone Chido roared across the the Indian Ocean archipelago of Mayotte on Saturday. 
But it’s feared hundreds or even thousands of people may have died.

items.title: Bird flu: Louisiana patient is first severe case and California declares emergency
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Health officials on Wednesday confirmed the first known severe illness in the U.S. caused by bird flu, and California's governor declared a state of emergency as the virus rampages through dairy cattle in the state. Read more: https://bit.ly/41Il4BF
